text: The intraoperative ultrasound image displays the biliary tree, with a flow box visible over the aorta.
An arrow points at the Yorda, highlighting the common bile duct and common hepatic duct. The portal vein is visible beneath both ducts, forming the 'Mickey Mouse sign' in the ultrasound image.

The ultrasound image shows the common bile duct as a tubular structure at the top, being followed inferiorly. The image is frozen to measure the common bile duct, highlighting any radiopaque structures indicative of stones.

The ultrasound image is unfrozen after measuring the common bile duct. The flow box is visible, showing no flow inside the common bile duct, which is positioned above the aorta. The portal vein is slightly to the right of the common bile duct.

The ultrasound image shows the flow box being moved inferiorly over the pulsating aorta, indicating blood flow. The common hepatic duct is visible, while the common bile duct shows no flow as it is followed inferiorly through the pancreas towards the duodenum.
